visual_checks: Look at components that perform the same function across multiple pages — are they labelled and identified in the same way each time? For example, is a search field always labelled 'Search' rather than 'Search' on one page and 'Find' on another?
Check icons used for the same function — does the same icon represent the same action throughout the site (e.g., a magnifying glass always means search, a shopping cart always means view cart)?
Compare button labels for repeated actions — is a 'Submit' button on one form called 'Send' on another form that performs the same action?
Look at download links, print buttons, social sharing icons, and other repeated functional components — are they consistently labelled and visually represented?
Check alt text for functional images and icons across pages — does the same icon have the same alt text each time it appears?
pass_conditions: Components that have the same functionality across multiple pages are identified consistently (same label, same icon, same alt text)
Search functionality is labelled identically wherever it appears (e.g., always 'Search', not 'Search' in one place and 'Find' in another)
Action buttons for equivalent functions use the same text label across the site (e.g., 'Add to Cart' is used consistently, not alternating with 'Buy Now' for the same action)
Icons serving the same function have consistent alt text and visual appearance across pages
Functional images (like logos that link to the homepage) have the same alt text on every page
fail_conditions: A search feature is labelled 'Search' on the homepage but 'Look Up' on interior pages — same function, different identification
A print icon has alt='Print this page' on one page and alt='Printer' on another page
Submit buttons on equivalent forms use different labels: 'Submit' on one, 'Go' on another, 'Send' on a third
A checkmark icon means 'Confirm' on one page but 'Complete' on another, creating inconsistent identification for the same function
The site logo links to the homepage but has different alt text on different pages (e.g., 'Home' vs 'Company Logo' vs 'Back to start')
na_conditions: The page is a single standalone page with no repeated functional components appearing on other pages
Only one page from the site was tested, making cross-page comparison of component identification impossible
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Only one page was captured, so consistent identification across pages cannot be evaluated
Alt text and labels for repeated components could not be determined from the available DOM data
common_mistakes: Using 'Search' and 'Find' interchangeably for the same search function on different pages
A 'Login' link in the header but 'Sign In' on the login page itself for the same action
Social media icons with different alt text on different pages (e.g., alt='Facebook' vs alt='Visit us on Facebook' vs alt='FB')
A contact form submit button labelled 'Send Message' but a newsletter form submit button labelled 'Submit' when both perform the same conceptual action of submitting a form
Inconsistent labelling of navigation toggles (e.g., 'Menu' on some pages, hamburger icon with alt='Navigation' on others)

examples.pass: The search input is labelled 'Search' with a magnifying glass icon (alt='Search') in the header on every page of the site — the label, icon, and alt text are identical everywhere
examples.fail: The main search box is labelled 'Search' on the homepage, 'Find Products' on the catalog page, and 'Look Up' on the support page — all three perform the same site-wide search but are identified differently
examples.partial: Most repeated components are consistently identified, but the 'Contact Us' link in the footer is labelled 'Get in Touch' on two pages while 'Contact Us' on all others

plain_meaning: Components that perform the same function across different pages of a site must be identified consistently. If a search button is called 'Search' on one page, it should not be called 'Find' on another. The focus is on the ACCESSIBLE LABEL and identification, not the visual styling.
scope_applies_to: Accessible names of buttons, links, and icons that repeat across pages with the same function
Icon-only controls whose alt text or aria-label identifies their purpose
Repeated form controls performing the same role across pages
Any control whose function is identical across pages
scope_does_not_apply_to: Visual styling, color, or pixel-level appearance (consistency here means identification, not style)
Controls whose function is genuinely different even if they look similar
Ordering of repeated components (that is SC 3.2.3)
A single-page review where no cross-page comparison is possible
borderline_calibration: Flag only when two controls with the same function have materially different accessible names across pages.
Minor wording differences that still convey the same function (for example 'Submit' vs 'Submit Form') are usually acceptable.
Do not flag different visual treatments when the accessible name is consistent.
Single-page reviews with nothing to compare across are Not Applicable or Not Enough Evidence.
